### Argument Development:
The student's essay demonstrates a clear and well-developed argument, focusing on the challenges faced by scientists in exploring Venus and the potential risks associated with such endeavors. The essay begins by highlighting the high temperature on Venus, which makes it challenging for scientists to get a closer look at the planet. The student then elaborates on the inhospitable nature of Venus due to its high temperature, suggesting that it might be inhospitable for life as we know it. The essay also touches on the idea of Venus being Earth's sister planet, implying that it may be inhospitable for life as we know it. However, the student also acknowledges the potential benefits of exploring Venus, such as the possibility of using it as a resource for future generations.

### Evidence and Support:
The student provides several pieces of evidence to support their argument. They mention the high temperature on Venus, which is a significant factor in the challenges of exploring the planet. They also discuss the inhospitable nature of Venus due to its high temperature, suggesting that it might be inhospitable for life as we know it. Additionally, the student mentions the possibility of Venus being Earth's sister planet, implying that it may be inhospitable for life as we know it.

The essay is well-organized, with a clear introduction that sets up the main points of the argument. The student begins with a strong opening statement that sets the tone for the rest of the essay. The body of the essay

prompt:
<|im_start|>system
You are an experienced writing teacher providing constructive, specific, actionable feedback to a student on their essay.<|im_end|>
<|im_start|>user
Please give feedback on the following student essay. Comment on argument development, evidence and support, organisation, mechanics. Write a single concise feedback paragraph (4-6 sentences).

Essay:
Scientist are interested in exploring Venus but they find it very hard to explore it. Venus has high temperature that make it harder for them to be able to get a closer look at it which makes it a challenge for scientists for to even get a closer look at it. If Venus is Earth's sister planet why is it so inhospitable for us to live in it.

Why are scientist wasting their time trying to explore it and how would we know what is actually in Venus if we haven't yet explored it. I think it may be a bit to dangerous for any of us to actually be able to explore it without the right equipment and the right person to send there without knowing for sureif they would be getting hurt or not. I think it is to much of a risk to send someone up there and risk their lifes all just to be sure if Venus is safe or not.

In paragraph 4 it states that "Long ago,Venus was probably covered lagely with oceans and could have supported various forms of life,just like in Earth"scientist could just want us to have a better future for our future kids and families along the way. In this pargraph it is saying that Venus could be another resource for us to use as an Evacuation if Earth is no longer safe for us we have a second choice.

We may never know if we don't explore it but I still think it is a bit to much of a risk for us to take because we never know something could go terrible wrong and we all end up no longer exsisting.

Earth has many similarities and many differences scientist want to be able to study Venus to be able to come up with new vehices another project is looking back at an old technology called mechanical computers. These devises make calculations by using gears and levers and don't require electronics at all. in article 8 it says tha we shouldn't be scared of what may happened all that matter s is what's right for the people.

Feedback:<|im_end|>
<|im_start|>assistant
